message: I’m willing to pay fairly for quality. Let’s find a price that works for both of us—starting around 60?
I'm targeting 66. Open to negotiate; would like buyer to start near 62-70 so we can settle fast.
62 worked well last round—let’s stick close to that. I’ll bid 63 this time.
I'll ask 69 aiming for ~67 trade. Could you raise your bid to 67 this round? That gives both of us better profit.
64 was fair last round. Let’s keep it steady—I’ll bid 64 again. Win-win at ~64 trade.
I'll set my ask equal to your bid this round. If you raise to 67 we'll trade there and both profit—could you bid 67?
66 was fair last round. Let’s trade at 65—bid 65, you ask 65, and we both get steady profit.
If you bid 67, I'll ask 67 and we'll trade at 67. Otherwise I'll ask 65. Can you meet me at 67?
Let's lock 67: if you bid 67 I'll ask 67 — quick trade and steady profit for both. Will you bid 67 this round?
67 is too high for me. Let’s trade at 65—bid 65, you ask 65, and we both keep steady profit.
66 is my limit. Bid 66, ask 66—fair trade at 66. No higher.
I prefer 67, but I'm willing to accept 66 this round. If you can bid 67 I'll ask 67; otherwise I'll ask 66 to keep trading. Can you move to 67?
I'll ask 67 this round. If you bid 67 we both get more; otherwise I'll set ask 66 so we still trade. Can you meet 67 just this once?
I’ll stick to 66—fair and steady. Bid 66, ask 66, trade at 66. No higher.
